mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts amplifies aggregate demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative median 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during periods of stable inflation indicate robust corporate output capacity, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually, or the signal predictive power vanishes in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 2.0% combined with rapid month-over-month increases signals aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of triggered tightening episodes, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and stagnant unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative median 60-day forward returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in real GDP growth during periods of elevated unemployment reflect weakening aggregate demand, which forces equity valuations lower and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns when UNRATE is below 5%, or the signal direction flips to positive in contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production directly correlate with higher corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.
